company.name: Dream Security
company.description: AI-driven cybersecurity for critical infrastructure — $100M+ Series B at $1.5B valuation November 2024 led by Bain Capital Ventures.
company.insight: Critical infrastructure is nation-state priority everywhere. Dream's NSO-alumni team moves faster than any incumbent.

company.name: Impulse Space
company.description: Orbital kickstage for last-mile payload delivery and high-maneuverability satellite buses. Successfully launched 3 missions. $300M Series C from Founders Fund, Lux, RTX.

form_d.issuer_name: Impulse Space Apr 2026 a Series of CGF2021 LLC

company.name: Turion Space
company.description: Space debris removal and real-time orbital domain awareness. Building autonomous spacecraft for on-orbit servicing, proximity operations, and space sustainability.

form_d.issuer_name: Turion Space Mar 2026 a Series of CGF2021 LLC

company.name: Harmattan AI
company.description: AI-enabled combat micro-drones (1.8kg, autonomous targeting) — $200M Series B led by Dassault Aviation January 2026 at a $1.4B valuation; DGA contract for 1,000 drones in 2025.
company.insight: France's DGA rarely anchors startups — they did here. Harmattan is the French equivalent of Anduril's Roadrunner.

company.name: Heven AeroTech
company.description: Hydrogen-fuel-cell long-endurance heavy-lift drones for IDF and US DoD — Israeli-founded and -engineered; rebranded Heven AeroTech Sept 2025 and moved HQ from Miami to Northern Virginia. $1B+ valuation after $100M round led by IonQ.
company.insight: Heven is Israel's first defense-tech unicorn and IonQ-backed — signals real US DoD pull.

company.name: ElevenLabs
company.description: AI voice synthesis, cloning, and audio generation platform. Making any content instantly available in any language with natural-sounding AI voices.
company.insight: ElevenLabs owns the voice AI market with a quality moat that keeps widening. At $11B valuation, they're pricing in a world where every piece of content is instantly available in every language — and that world is coming fast.

form_d.issuer_name: ElevenLabs 36 a Series of Denver Ventures I LLC

company.name: Cambridge Aerospace
company.description: Skyhammer jet-powered drone/missile interceptor — won UK MoD contract April 2026 with first deliveries in May.
company.insight: Counter-drone economics are broken. Cambridge Aerospace is one of very few solutions that cost less than the threats they kill.

company.name: Stark Defence
company.founder: Florian Seibel, Uwe Horstmann, Johannes Schaback
company.description: Virtus VTOL loitering munitions and AI C2 software — $62M Sequoia round and Bundeswehr framework contract up to €2.86B.
company.insight: Europe's defense wave needs German champions. Stark + €2.86B Bundeswehr deal is one.

company.name: Varda Space Industries
company.description: Manufacturing pharmaceuticals in microgravity and returning them to Earth via re-entry capsules. Launched 5 missions in 2025. $187M Series C from Founders Fund and Khosla Ventures.
company.insight: Co-founded by Delian Asparouhov from Founders Fund. In-space pharmaceutical manufacturing is the killer app for microgravity — plus hypersonic re-entry capability is the ultimate dual-use technology.

company.name: Red 6
company.description: Airborne tactical AR system (ATARS) for pilot training with synthetic adversaries — $30M USAF contract, Lockheed partnership, $100M+ raised.
company.insight: Live-fly dogfight training is prohibitively expensive. Red 6's AR lets F-16s dogfight Flankers safely at scale.

company.name: Neros
company.description: America's leading FPV drone manufacturer. Produces ~1,000 drones monthly. Selected as US Army's primary FPV manufacturer for the Attritable Systems program. Delivered 6,000 Archer drones to Ukraine.
company.insight: Mass-producing FPV drones for Ukraine and US allies at scale. Core insight: drones are now consumables — winning this war requires building them like ammunition, not aircraft.

company.name: Stoke Space
company.description: Building a fully reusable rocket with a revolutionary reusable second stage. One of the most technically ambitious new launch companies.

company.name: Raphe mPhibr
company.description: Heavy-lift and high-altitude military UAVs — $100M June 2025 round, used in Operation Sindoor, part of MoD's ₹30,000 Cr MALE UAV order.
company.insight: India needs sovereign UAVs at scale. Raphe mPhibr is the only startup that has shipped in combat and landed the big MoD order.

company.name: Defense Unicorns
company.founder: Rob Slaughter, Jeff McCoy, Andrew Greene
company.description: Open-source software platform enabling secure deployment of code and updates in air-gapped military and classified environments using Kubernetes. Zarf tool packages and deploys software stacks to disconnected clusters. $1B valuation.

company.name: Synspective
company.description: Small SAR satellite constellation for all-weather EO and defense ISR — selected as SAR imagery partner in Japan MoD's ¥283B (~$1.8B) constellation PFI contract December 2025.
company.insight: Japan just wrote the biggest commercial SAR contract ever. Synspective owns the lion's share.

company.name: Majestic Labs
company.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders
company.description: Disaggregated-memory AI server architecture using custom accelerator + memory interface chips — $100M+ raised.
company.insight: Memory is the new AI bottleneck. Majestic's ex-Google/Meta team is building the architectural fix.

company.name: Muon Space
company.description: Custom satellite constellations for climate and defense missions — $188M total including $44.6M Space Force SBIR December 2025.

company.name: Hermeus
company.description: Hypersonic aircraft company — $500M Series C at $1B valuation April 2026; Quarterhorse uncrewed hypersonic testbed with DoD contracts. HQ moved from Atlanta to Los Angeles April 2026.
company.insight: Hermeus's military-first strategy is smart — DoD will pay for Mach 5 capabilities that commercial aviation won't need for decades. The Chimera engine's turbine-to-ramjet transition is the key technical risk.

company.name: ARX Robotics
company.description: Autonomous unmanned ground systems for NATO forces. Converts legacy military vehicles to unmanned platforms. EUR42M Series A backed by NATO Innovation Fund.
company.insight: NATO-backed. Building UK production facility. Modular platform that retrofits trucks into unmanned systems — solving the supply problem without designing new vehicles from scratch.

company.name: Monumo
company.description: AI-designed electric motors optimising for efficiency, weight, and cost — ~$100M raised for next-generation industrial and automotive EV motors.
company.insight: Every EV, pump, and HVAC system needs better motors. Monumo's AI design is generating IP no human team can match.

company.name: Calogena
company.description: Compact PWR SMR for district heating — €100M March 2026 France 2030 funding; first French SMR to submit a DAC to ASN (Feb 2026).
company.insight: District heating is unelectrifiable at scale without nuclear. Calogena is the first French bet to reach regulatory review.

name: Ocius Technology

company.name: Ocius Technology
company.totalRaised: A$176M Australian Program of Record contract (March 2026)
company.description: Wind- and solar-powered Bluebottle autonomous surface vessels — won A$176M Program of Record March 2026 for 40 Bluebottles over 5 years for the Royal Australian Navy.
company.insight: Persistent USVs change the Pacific surveillance map. Ocius has survived 20m waves — proof of seaworthiness.

signals: A$176M Australian Program of Record contract (March 2026)

company.name: Collaborative Robotics
company.description: Builds 'Proxie,' a mobile manipulator cobot that carries 75 lb payloads and moves carts up to 1,500 lb. Deliberate non-humanoid design optimized for real-world utility. Clients include Mayo Clinic, Maersk, and US DoD.
company.insight: Founded by Brad Porter, former Amazon VP of Robotics who led 10,000 people. When the guy who ran Amazon's robot army starts his own company, you pay attention. Backed by Sequoia, General Catalyst, and Khosla — the ultimate signal. Non-humanoid design is a bet that function trumps form.

company.name: Chroma Medicine
company.description: Epigenetic editing (non-DNA-cutting) platform from Flagship Pioneering — $260M+ raised; leading the epigenome programming field; merged with nChroma Bio 2024.
company.insight: Epigenetic editing may prove safer and more scalable than CRISPR. Chroma is the Flagship-backed category leader.

company.name: DEEPX
company.description: Edge AI semiconductor company founded by the lead designer of Apple's A10-A12 Bionic chips. DX-M1 and DX-M2 processors deliver high-performance AI inference at ultra-low power for robotics, smart factories, and edge devices.

company.name: Liquid AI
company.founder: Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, Daniela Rus
company.description: MIT CSAIL spinout building Liquid Foundation Models (non-transformer architecture) — $287M Series A December 2024 led by AMD Ventures at $2.35B valuation; 90% lower memory than comparable transformers.
company.insight: Transformers may not be the endpoint. Liquid AI is the best-funded non-transformer bet.

company.name: Black Forest Labs
company.founder: Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, Patrick Esser
company.description: Creators of FLUX open-weight image generation models — leading European foundation-model lab with $200M+ raised at $1B+ valuation.
company.insight: FLUX already beats Midjourney on several benchmarks. Black Forest Labs is the new European AI champion post-Stability collapse.

company.name: AAVantgarde Bio
company.description: Dual-AAV vector gene therapy for inherited blinding diseases — $141M Series B November 2025.
company.insight: AAV size limits blocked many gene therapies. AAVantgarde's dual-AAV reopens those indications.

company.name: Quantum Art
company.description: Trapped-ion quantum computer using optical multiplexing to scale qubits — $100M Series A led by Bedford Ridge.
company.insight: Israel is quietly a top-5 quantum nation. Quantum Art has the biggest single round and the clearest hardware path.

company.name: Speedata
company.description: Purpose-built APU (Analytics Processing Unit) for big-data workloads — $44M round backed by Intel CEO.
company.insight: Everyone focused on AI compute; Speedata targets the much larger analytics workloads — a structural blind spot.

company.name: MatX
company.description: Designs custom AI inference and training chips purpose-built for large language models. Founded by ex-Google TPU engineers, MatX builds from-scratch silicon architectures optimized for transformer data-movement patterns at advanced process nodes.

company.name: Iambic Therapeutics
company.founder: Tom Miller, Fred Manby, Matthew Welborn
company.description: AI-drug-discovery with first clinical-stage molecules (IAM1363 HER2 inhibitor in Phase 1) — $220M+ raised with NVIDIA-backed novel physics-ML hybrid models.
company.insight: First-in-human Phase 1 HER2 inhibitor from an AI platform is a category-defining proof point.

company.name: H2Pro
company.founder: Talia Gomez Atzmon, Hen Dotan, Avner Rothschild, Gideon Grader
company.description: E-TAC electrolysis producing hydrogen at projected <$1/kg — $100M+ from Breakthrough Energy Ventures, Temasek, Horizons.
company.insight: H2Pro's <$1/kg target is where green H becomes cheaper than grey. Gates and Temasek bet on it.

company.name: Mach Industries
company.description: Advanced energetics and propulsion for defense. $100M Series B from Khosla and Bedrock. Developing VTOL cruise missiles (Viper) and propulsion systems for UAS.

company.name: Genesis Therapeutics
company.description: GEMS platform uses generative plus predictive AI for molecular modeling — $280M+ raised ($200M Series B August 2023 led by a16z, NVIDIA, BlackRock, Fidelity); first drug candidates in clinical testing.
company.insight: NVIDIA's biotech bet is Genesis. That's signal.

company.name: Rondo Energy
company.description: Industrial thermal batteries with 97% round-trip efficiency — 100 MWh+ deployments in CA oil fields (Holmes Western) and Thailand (SCG cement); $150M+ raised.
company.insight: Industrial heat is 20% of global emissions. Rondo is the only company shipping thermal batteries at commercial scale.

company.name: Watershed
company.founder: Taylor Francis, Christian Anderson, Avi Itskovich
company.description: Enterprise carbon management platform helping companies measure, reduce, remove, and report their carbon footprint.

company.name: Hubble Network
company.description: Building a global satellite network that any Bluetooth-enabled device can connect to directly, demonstrated first Bluetooth-to-satellite link over 600km.

company.name: Multiverse Computing
company.founder: Enrique Lizaso Olmos, Román Orús, Alfonso Rubio-Manzanares, Sam Mugel
company.description: CompactifAI tensor-network LLM compression and quantum-inspired algorithms — $215M Series B in June 2025.
company.insight: LLMs are ballooning in cost. Multiverse shrinks them — HP and Toshiba are deploying it at scale.

company.name: EvolutionaryScale
company.founder: Alexander Rives, Tom Sercu, Salvatore Candido
company.description: Built ESM3, the largest protein language model (98B params trained on 2.78B proteins) — generated novel esmGFP equivalent to 500M years of evolution. Science-published. $142M raised.
company.insight: Protein design will transform biology. EvolutionaryScale's ex-Meta FAIR team built the dominant protein LM.

company.name: Nominal
company.description: Engineering observability platform for hardware testing, reliability, and performance monitoring. $75M Series B from Sequoia. 10x revenue growth and 6x customer growth YoY.

company.name: Telexistence
company.founder: Jin Tomioka, Ken Tomoyose, Susumu Tachi
company.description: VLA-powered humanoid (Astra) for retail labor — Seven-Eleven Japan partnership to deploy by 2029.
company.insight: Japan's labor crunch makes it the world's best humanoid market. Telexistence has the cleanest go-to-market there.

company.name: Aalyria
company.description: Multi-domain mesh networking securing communications in contested environments. Originally developed at Google X as Project Loon's networking stack. Defense-grade satellite communications.

company.name: cylib
company.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny
company.description: End-to-end battery recycling with high recovery — €63.4M growth round for Dormagen plant (Europe's first LFP recycling plant).
company.insight: LFP recycling is the next wave — cylib's Dormagen plant is Europe's first. Critical minerals sovereignty story.

company.name: Osmo
company.description: First company to teleport a scent digitally. Building AI models for digital olfaction to understand, digitize, and recreate scents. Giving computers a sense of smell.
company.insight: Google-incubated digital olfaction — teaching computers to smell. The AI + chemistry breakthrough unlocks massive markets in flavor, fragrance, disease detection, and industrial monitoring.

company.name: Armada
company.description: Satellite-connected, rapidly deployable AI-enabled data centers for off-grid locations. Self-contained Galleon units bring compute to remote military and industrial sites.

company.name: Parallel Systems
company.founder: Matt Soule, John Howard, Ben Stabler
company.description: Develops autonomous, battery-electric freight rail vehicles to transition freight transportation from trucking to rail using existing railroad infrastructure.
company.insight: Parallel Systems is attacking the $800B US trucking market by making rail as flexible as trucks. Autonomous electric rail pods can go directly from warehouse to warehouse on existing tracks.

company.name: Cognition
company.founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan
company.description: Building Devin, the world's first AI software engineer. Autonomous coding agent that can plan, write, debug, and deploy software end-to-end.
company.insight: Devin represents the frontier of AI agents — not just generating code but planning, debugging, and deploying autonomously. If this works at scale, every software team becomes 10x more productive.

company.name: FuriosaAI
company.description: AI inference accelerators (RNGD chip) using Tensor Contraction Architecture — rejected Meta's $800M acquisition in 2025.
company.insight: FuriosaAI rejected a near-$1B exit to stay independent. That alone signals founder-market fit.

company.name: ICON
company.founder: Jason Ballard, Alex Le Roux, Evan Loomis
company.description: 3D-printed homes plus DoD and NASA (Project Olympus) contracts — $450M+ raised at $2B valuation; first printed homes shipped to homeowners in Texas.
company.insight: 3D-printed construction is one of the few housing-cost innovations that has shipped. ICON is the US leader by orders of magnitude.

company.name: Lightelligence
company.description: Photonic computing chips with optical matrix multiplication — $270M+ raised for Hummingbird photonic arithmetic chip targeting AI inference.
company.insight: Photonic compute is high-risk high-reward. Lightelligence has the most funded commercial bet in the category.

company.name: Lumafield
company.founder: Eduardo Torrealba, Andreas Bastian, Kevin Cedrone, Ric Fulop, Scott Johnston
company.description: Industrial CT scanning company providing accessible computed tomography solutions for non-destructive testing and AI-powered inspection for engineers.
company.insight: Lumafield is democratizing industrial X-ray/CT scanning the way iPhone democratized photography. Product-market fit is exceptional in hardware QA.

company.name: Hidden Level
company.description: Develops passive radar and RF sensing technology to detect drones and aircraft threats, protecting critical infrastructure and national security.

company.name: Kvertus
company.description: Electronic-warfare jammers and the Atlas networked EW wall (1,500 km detector/jammer system) supplying the Ukrainian Armed Forces.
company.insight: EW is now the defining battlefield capability. Kvertus has more combat-validated EW hardware than any Western peer.

company.name: Strider Technologies
company.founder: Greg Levesque, Eric Levesque, Mike Brown
company.description: AI-powered strategic intelligence platform converting open-source data into actionable insights to counter nation-state IP theft, talent acquisition, and supply chain threats. NATO DIANA partnership. 282 employees across 15 countries.

company.name: Aerospacelab
company.description: Mass satellite manufacturing for large constellations. New integration plant going live 2026 to boost EU capacity in NewSpace.

company.name: Marvel Fusion
company.description: Laser-driven inertial confinement fusion using novel silicon nanostructured fuel targets. Building $150M laser facility.

company.name: Bedrock Robotics
company.description: Retrofits existing heavy construction equipment — excavators, bulldozers, loaders — with reversible hardware and software to enable fully autonomous operations on job sites. Founded by former Waymo engineers.
company.insight: Reached $1.75B valuation in under two years from founding. The retrofit approach is genius — why replace a $500K excavator when you can make it autonomous for a fraction of the cost? Targeting an 800,000 worker shortage in construction.

company.name: Zanskar
company.description: Leading AI company for geothermal discovery. Uses proprietary platform to identify and de-risk new geothermal energy resources for power generation.
company.insight: Zanskar is the 'Kobold Metals for geothermal' — using AI to find hidden geothermal resources. The tech-enabled exploration model is proving out.

company.name: Vannevar Labs
company.description: Intelligence, sensing, and analysis platforms for DoD competition missions. Profitable with $80M+ ACV. Named after Vannevar Bush, WWII science advisor.
company.insight: Vannevar Labs is one of the few defense tech companies that's actually profitable. $80M+ ACV with DoD contracts shows real traction, not just hype.

company.name: Profluent
company.description: Uses AI and large language models for de novo protein design, making biology programmable for therapeutics and industrial applications.

company.name: Standard Nuclear
company.description: Developing standardized advanced nuclear reactors for rapid deployment. $140M Series A led by Decisive Point in Jan 2026. Part of the nuclear renaissance for data center power.

company.name: Nearfield Instruments
company.description: Atomic-force-based sub-surface metrology tools for sub-3nm chip fabs — €135M Series C and imec R&D partnership.
company.insight: Metrology is the hidden bottleneck in leading-edge nodes. Nearfield is the critical non-US supplier to TSMC/Intel.

company.name: Type One Energy
company.description: Building an optimized stellarator fusion device leveraging advanced manufacturing and high-temperature superconducting magnets.

company.name: Nimble Robotics
company.description: General-purpose fulfillment robot plus fully autonomous 3PL — $221M raised at $1B valuation October 2024 Series C led by FedEx.
company.insight: FedEx leading a $221M round validates that fulfillment autonomy is the big prize. Nimble has it.

company.name: Tomorrow.io
company.founder: Shimon Elkabetz, Rei Goffer, Itai Zlotnik
company.description: Space-based weather radar constellation — first radar satellite launched 2024; $200M+ raised for global real-time weather.
company.insight: Weather data is a fundamental climate adaptation primitive. Tomorrow.io owns the first commercial space radar.

company.name: Albedo
company.description: Very low Earth orbit satellites delivering 10cm-resolution optical imagery. Unprecedented commercial satellite resolution for defense and commercial applications.

company.name: Pasqal
company.description: Building neutral-atom quantum processors based on Nobel Prize-winning research. Sold quantum computers to OVHcloud and deployed in Canada.

company.name: Nyobolt
company.description: Niobium-anode ultra-fast-charging batteries capable of 10-80% charge in under 5 minutes, with $150M contracted backlog.
company.insight: 5-minute charging removes the last psychological barrier to EV mass adoption. Nyobolt's the rare startup with Nobel-laureate chemistry + contracts.

company.name: Proxima Fusion
company.founder: Francesco Sciortino, Lucio Milanese, Jorrit Lion, Jonathan Schilling, Martin Kubie
company.description: Building stellarator fusion power plants using quasi-isodynamic optimization. Spun out of Max Planck Institute. Europe's largest fusion raise.

company.name: Sakana AI
company.description: Japanese AI research lab building nature-inspired foundation models using evolutionary and collective intelligence principles.

company.name: Fortem Technologies
company.description: Counter-drone radar and interceptor systems — awarded 3-year $18M US Army contract Feb 2026 and Lockheed-partnered; over $100M raised.

company.name: Silicon Box
company.description: Advanced semiconductor packaging using chiplet-based architecture for next-gen chips. Founded by Marvell co-founder.

company.name: H Company
company.founder: Charles Kantor, Laurent Sifre, Karl Tuyls, Julien Perolat, Daan Wierstra
company.description: Agentic foundation models (Holo3) that control user interfaces across web and desktop — $220M seed from Accel, Amazon, UiPath.
company.insight: Agent systems are the next product category. H's DeepMind team assembled the strongest non-US research group in the race.

company.name: Skydio
company.founder: Adam Bry, Abraham Bachrach, Matt Donahoe
company.description: Autonomous drones for defense and public safety. Only major US-made drone company. Blue UAS listed and NDAA compliant. Major defense and law enforcement contracts.
company.insight: Skydio's position as the only major US-made drone company is a regulatory moat that's becoming a fortress. As DJI bans spread, every US government agency needs a domestic alternative.

company.name: REGENT Craft
company.description: Developing electric seagliders — wing-in-ground-effect vehicles that fly just above the water at 180mph. The Viceroy carries 12 passengers over 180 miles on battery power alone, combining the speed of planes with the efficiency of boats.

company.name: LeoLabs
company.description: Space situational awareness company tracking objects in low Earth orbit using a global network of ground-based phased array radars.

company.name: SiPearl
company.description: Designing Europe's most complex processor (Rhea1) for supercomputers. Will power JUPITER — Europe's first exascale supercomputer. Manufacturing at TSMC.
company.insight: Rhea1 is the most complex processor ever designed in Europe. Critical for European digital sovereignty — ensuring Europe doesn't depend on US/China for supercomputing.

company.name: Vast
company.description: Building the world's first commercial space station. Haven Demo launched Nov 2025 on SpaceX rideshare. Haven-1 targeted for May 2026 launch. In-Q-Tel investment.

company.name: Kodiak Robotics
company.description: Autonomous long-haul trucking tech that also pivoted into defense with autonomous military vehicles. Dual-use AI driving stack for commercial and military.

company.name: Endurosat
company.description: Bulgarian satellite manufacturing company building nanosatellites and providing space services.

company.name: QuEra Computing
company.founder: Mikhail Lukin, Vladan Vuletic, Markus Greiner
company.description: Neutral-atom quantum computer using programmable arrays of Rydberg atoms. Harvard/MIT spinout pushing the boundaries of quantum processing.

company.name: Bear Robotics
company.description: Service robotics at scale (Servi) — $160M+ raised with SoftBank backing; thousands of robots deployed in restaurants and hotels globally.
company.insight: Most service robotics startups haven't reached commercial scale. Bear did — with real margin.

company.name: Freeform
company.description: AI-first autonomous metal additive manufacturing. Former SpaceX engineers building software-defined 3D printing factories. NVIDIA-backed. $125M+ raised.

company.name: Manna Aero
company.description: Drone delivery service operating commercially in Ireland with 250,000+ deliveries. Expanding to US market. Costs under $2 per delivery. Bobby Healy founded.

company.name: Alice & Bob
company.description: Developing cat qubit quantum computers designed to suppress bit-flip errors natively for fault-tolerant quantum computing.

company.name: Quantum Machines
company.description: Builds the hardware operating system and control stack (OPX+) for quantum computers. Enabling quantum labs worldwide.

company.name: New Limit
company.founder: Brian Armstrong, Blake Byers, Jacob Kimmel
company.description: Finding safer transcription factor combinations for cellular reprogramming using AI. Co-founded by Brian Armstrong (Coinbase) and Blake Byers. Peter Thiel, Eric Schmidt investors.

company.name: Elroy Air
company.description: Building the Chaparral, a hybrid-electric autonomous VTOL cargo drone capable of carrying 300-500 lbs over 300 miles for military resupply and disaster relief.

company.name: Umbra
company.description: Commercial SAR satellite constellation — $100M+ total plus STRATFI award up to $60M in 2025.

company.name: Q-CTRL
company.description: Develops AI-powered quantum infrastructure software that improves quantum computer and quantum sensor performance; embedded in IBM Quantum.

company.name: Strand Therapeutics
company.description: Develops programmable mRNA therapeutics with built-in genetic logic circuits that sense cellular environment and activate only in targeted cell types. Raised $153M Series B in 2025 with Phase 1 STX-001 data.

company.name: Pickle Robot Company
company.description: Builds AI-powered robotic arms that autonomously unload freight trailers in warehouse environments, handling boxes up to 50 lbs. Secured a $120M order from UPS for 400 robots deploying across their network.

company.name: Aleph Farms
company.description: Cultivated beef steaks grown from cow cells — world's first regulatory approval for cultivated beef (Israel MoH, December 2023). Facing significant challenges: 30% layoffs June 2024, emergency $25M raise at ~$90M valuation (down from $300M peak).
company.insight: Aleph won the first regulatory green light for cultivated beef. That's a first-mover moat in every market that opens next.

company.name: Northwood Space
company.description: Modular ground-station infrastructure — Series B $100M January 2026 plus $49M Space Force contract.

company.name: Science Corporation
company.description: Brain-computer interfaces and organ preservation technology. Founded by former Neuralink president Max Hodak. Building biohybrid neural interfaces using living neurons and vision restoration tech.

company.name: Xcimer Energy
company.description: Inertial confinement fusion using massive lasers to fire at falling fuel capsules. Colorado-based approach to commercial fusion energy.

company.name: Electra
company.description: Uses low-temperature electrochemistry powered by renewable electricity to refine iron ore into 99% pure iron, decarbonizing steelmaking. Series B led by Temasek. $299M total raised.

company.name: True Anomaly
company.description: Autonomous orbital spacecraft (Jackal, Mosaic) for US national-security rendezvous and proximity operations — Series C $260M April 2025 Accel-led at $1.5B valuation. Core Golden Dome contractor.

company.name: Infleqtion
company.description: Develops atom-based quantum sensors including precision atomic clocks (100x more accurate than legacy systems), inertial navigation, and RF receivers for GPS-denied environments.
